title: After-Hours Archive Staging and Exfiltration to a Typosquat Domain
short_description: A workstation transferred 2.3GB to a 9-day-old lookalike domain at 2 AM via a scheduled task, while its assigned user was off-site.

alert.rule_name: DLP - Anomalous Outbound Data Transfer (After Hours)
alert.description: Host corp-ws-221 transferred 2.3GB over HTTPS to files.sharepoint-update-cdn.com between 02:00-02:45, roughly 57x this host's daily baseline.
alert.raw: 06/13/2026 02:14:00 DLP-ALERT host=corp-ws-221 user=FROTHLY\jchen dest=files.sharepoint-update-cdn.com bytes_out=2469135872 rule="Anomalous Outbound Data Transfer" severity=medium

steps.title: Compare last night's outbound traffic for corp-ws-221 against its baseline
steps.spl: search index=network host="corp-ws-221" dest="files.sharepoint-update-cdn.com" earliest="2026-06-13T02:00:00" latest="2026-06-13T02:45:00" | stats sum(bytes_out) as bytes_out_last_night | append [search index=network host="corp-ws-221" earliest=-30d@d latest=now | stats avg(bytes_out) as avg_daily_bytes_out by date_mday | stats avg(avg_daily_bytes_out) as baseline_avg_bytes_out]

steps.title: Check domain registration age and allowlist status for files.sharepoint-update-cdn.com
steps.spl: search index=dns query="files.sharepoint-update-cdn.com" | head 1 | lookup whois_lookup.csv domain as query OUTPUT registrar, domain_age_days | lookup approved_saas_domains.csv domain as query OUTPUT is_approved

events.note: Domain is a 9-day-old, unapproved, near-duplicate of the legitimate 'sharepoint.com' - a classic typosquat pattern.

steps.title: Search process activity on corp-ws-221 immediately before the transfer
steps.spl: search index=endpoint host="corp-ws-221" earliest="2026-06-13T01:50:00" latest="2026-06-13T02:00:00" Image="*7z.exe" | table _time, host, User, Image, CommandLine

events.note: Archive size (~2.31GB) matches the 2.3GB transferred to files.sharepoint-update-cdn.com two minutes later.

steps.title: Check jchen's badge and VPN activity overnight

result.title: Senior Financial Analyst
result.badge_log_last_event: Badged OUT of HQ at 2026-06-12T18:32:00Z (prior evening)
result.notes: corp-ws-221 remained associated with the corporate LAN DHCP scope all night (no VPN/remote session was established), yet the session was active under jchen's account at 02:00 - jchen was physically off-site and not connected remotely at the time of the activity.

steps.title: Search for scheduled task / persistence artifacts on corp-ws-221
steps.spl: search index=endpoint host="corp-ws-221" EventCode=4698 earliest=-14d | table _time, host, Account_Name, TaskName, TaskContent

events.note: Task was created 5 days ago (4 days after the lookalike domain was registered) and runs as jchen, but jchen has no recollection of creating it - likely planted via a compromised session or malicious script.

ground_truth.narrative: A scheduled task named 'OneDrive Sync Helper', planted on corp-ws-221 five days ago (four days after the attacker registered the typosquat domain files.sharepoint-update-cdn.com), fires nightly at 02:00, archives the entire \\fileserver\Finance\ share, and uploads it to that domain. Last night it ran as designed: a 2.31GB archive was created at 01:58 and 2.3GB was exfiltrated to the lookalike domain over the next 45 minutes. The account of record, jchen, was physically off-site (badged out at 18:32 the prior evening) with no VPN session active, meaning the task executed autonomously under a dormant session rather than via direct user action. This points to a compromised endpoint/credential being used as a foothold for sustained, automated exfiltration of sensitive Finance data, rather than a one-off insider action - though both the technical compromise and a potential insider angle should be investigated in parallel.
recommended_actions: Isolate corp-ws-221 from the network and capture a forensic image before remediation.
Disable and extract the 'OneDrive Sync Helper' scheduled task for analysis, then hunt for the same task name/pattern fleet-wide.
Block files.sharepoint-update-cdn.com at DNS, proxy, and firewall, and submit it to threat intel as a confirmed exfil C2.
Force-reset jchen's credentials and review all activity on this account for the last 14 days across all hosts.
Engage the insider-threat playbook (Legal/HR) in parallel with the technical response, given sensitive Finance data was staged and exfiltrated.
Audit \\fileserver\Finance\ access logs for any other host exhibiting the same archive-then-upload pattern.
